description:
## Goal

Two small cleanups in `src/fetcher/monitoring.cr` that the code
review flagged as "should-fix":

1. `Monitoring.stuck?(max_age_seconds)` currently returns
   `false` when the supervisor has NEVER run a cycle
   (`@@last_refresh_start == 0`). The contract is
   "false = healthy, true = stuck", and "never started" is
   actually a third state. Currently safe because
   `app_bootstrap.cr:103` only calls `stuck?` after at least
   one refresh attempt, but the implicit contract is fragile.

2. `Monitoring.attempt_recovery` has a redundant check at
   the end: `old_value = @@last_refresh_start.get; return false
   if old_value == 0` — the top of the method already returned
   for `start_time == 0`, and `start_time` and `old_value` were
   read from the same atomic. Pre-existing (carried over from
   the original `RefreshHealthMonitor.attempt_recovery`), but
   it's a 1-line cleanup while we're in the file.

## Why now

Both are in the same file and the same method cluster. Cheap
to fix together. The cleanup also makes the contract more
explicit, which helps anyone who later calls these methods
from a different entry point (e.g. a future health-check
endpoint).

## Design

For the `stuck?` never-started case, the safest fix is to keep
the current behavior (return `false`) but document it
explicitly:

```crystal
def self.stuck?(max_age_seconds : Int32) : Bool
  start_time = @@last_refresh_start.get
  # Never-started is treated as "not stuck" — there is no
  # cycle to time out. Callers that need a tri-state
  # distinction can check `Monitoring.status[:last_start] != 0`
  # before calling `stuck?`.
  return false if start_time == 0
  ...
end
```

The `attempt_recovery` cleanup:

```crystal
def self.attempt_recovery : Bool
  start_time = @@last_refresh_start.get
  return false if start_time == 0
  last_complete = @@last_refresh_complete.get
  return false if last_complete > start_time
  @@last_refresh_start.set(0)
  Log.for("quickheadlines.feed").info { "Monitoring: atomic recovery performed" }
  true
end
```

## Acceptance

- The comment on `stuck?` explains the never-started case
- The redundant `old_value` line is removed from
  `attempt_recovery`
- The merged-module spec (added by the parallel
  quickhea-X1 ticket) covers both behaviors
- Full suite passes
- `just nix-build` succeeds
title: Clarify Monitoring.stuck? semantics and clean up attempt_recovery
